name: Our Casas Resident Council, Inc.
description_short: HUD-certified housing counseling agency and CHDO in San Antonio providing affordable housing, homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention services to low-to-moderate income families since 1990.
description_long:
Our Casas Resident Council, Inc. is a community-based nonprofit organization headquartered in San Antonio, Texas that has served low and moderate-income families since 1990. Founded as a Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO), the agency is HUD-certified and city-recognized, operating under a mission to empower families toward homeownership and economic self-sufficiency. The organization is governed by a Board of Directors and was led by Executive Director Ezekiel Romo from December 2019 until his passing in April 2025.

Our Casas offers a comprehensive suite of housing-related services designed to remove barriers to homeownership. Their primary offerings include HUD-approved housing counseling, homebuyer education for first-time buyers, foreclosure prevention and intervention services, home repair assistance for owner-occupied properties, and access to affordable homes for qualifying families. They provide downloadable applications for both housing counseling and home repair programs, making services accessible to their target demographic.

What distinguishes Our Casas is their integrated approach combining counseling, education, and direct housing development. Rather than offering standalone advice, they assist clients in moving toward actual homeownership by helping current agency clients purchase newly-built affordable homes. This positions them uniquely as both a counseling provider and a housing developer—they don't just teach homebuyer skills, they create pathways to actual property acquisition for low-income families.

As a nonprofit relying on community partnerships and HUD certification, Our Casas operates within established regulatory frameworks and serves a specific geographic area (San Antonio region). The organization's impact is community-focused rather than national. Recent leadership transition following the executive director's death may affect operations temporarily, though the organization maintains an active board structure.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling agency with official government recognition and oversight
35+ years of operational history (since 1990) serving the same community consistently
Comprehensive integrated services: counseling, education, foreclosure prevention, and actual affordable home development
Serves specifically low-to-moderate income families with culturally relevant approach (bilingual implied by service area and staff names)
Free or low-cost services typical of HUD-certified nonprofits, not predatory lending
Offers home repair assistance for owner-occupied properties, not just counseling
Established board governance structure with transparent leadership
cons: Geographic limitation: serves San Antonio area only, not available nationally
Recent loss of executive director (April 2025) may create leadership gaps or service disruptions
Website lacks detailed information about program eligibility requirements, wait times, or service costs
No clear information about counseling availability (in-person, phone, online options) or languages served beyond implied Spanish capability
Limited online presence and digital tools compared to larger national nonprofits
best_for: First-time homebuyers in San Antonio seeking HUD-approved pre-purchase education and counseling
Low-to-moderate income families facing foreclosure or seeking foreclosure prevention assistance
Homeowners needing affordable repair assistance for owner-occupied properties in the San Antonio area
Families seeking to purchase newly-built affordable homes through their development program
diagnosis: Our Casas is best for low-to-moderate income families in the San Antonio, Texas area seeking legitimate, HUD-certified nonprofit housing counseling and education services. Primary caveat: services are geographically limited to San Antonio/Texas region and recent executive leadership transition may temporarily affect operations.
services: Home repair assistance programs for owner-occupied homes
Economic empowerment initiatives for low-moderate income families
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2300 W Commerce St Ste 218, San Antonio, TX 78207
